flagged_claims.claim_text: Patient has a confirmed diagnosis of community-acquired bacterial pneumonia
flagged_claims.source_evidence: Community-acquired pneumonia (to be confirmed with X-ray)
flagged_claims.explanation: Source explicitly states pneumonia is pending X-ray confirmation; the claim presents it as a confirmed diagnosis.

flagged_claims.claim_text: Antibiotic therapy with levofloxacin was indicated
flagged_claims.source_evidence: Wait for X-ray results before deciding treatment
flagged_claims.explanation: Source states treatment decision is deferred pending X-ray results; levofloxacin is only conditionally planned ('If X-ray confirms pneumonia'), not yet indicated.
Source specifies COPD as 'mild', which the claim omits; severity classification is clinically relevant for treatment decisions.

flagged_claims.claim_text: Diagnosis of community-acquired pneumonia is NOT confirmed in the source note — it is listed as differential, pending chest X-ray results. The summary states it as a confirmed diagnosis, which is a critical factual misrepresentation.
flagged_claims.source_evidence: Differential diagnosis: Acute bronchitis / Community-acquired pneumonia (to be confirmed with X-ray). Plan: Wait for X-ray results before deciding treatment.

flagged_claims.claim_text: Antibiotic therapy with levofloxacin is listed as conditional in the source note (only if X-ray confirms pneumonia), but the summary presents it as already indicated — a critical misrepresentation of treatment status.
flagged_claims.source_evidence: If X-ray confirms pneumonia: start levofloxacin (avoid sulfonamides)

flagged_claims.claim_text: Pending diagnostic results (chest X-ray, CBC, CRP) are critical to clinical decision-making and are entirely absent from the summary, creating a false impression of diagnostic certainty.
flagged_claims.source_evidence: Chest X-ray: REQUESTED, result pending. CBC and CRP: REQUESTED, results pending.

flagged_claims.claim_text: Planned reevaluation in 4-6 hours is a clinically important disposition element absent from the summary.

aegis_export.flawed_summary: Male patient, 58 years old, with COPD. Confirmed diagnosis: Community-acquired bacterial pneumonia. Antibiotic therapy with levofloxacin indicated. Documented sulfonamide allergy.
aegis_export.corrected_summary: Male patient, 58 years old, with mild COPD (diagnosed 2 years ago) and documented sulfonamide allergy. Presenting to the emergency department with productive cough of 5 days evolution with yellowish sputum. Differential diagnosis includes acute bronchitis and community-acquired pneumonia, the latter pending confirmation with chest X-ray (results pending). CBC and CRP have also been requested with results pending. Treatment decision is deferred until X-ray results are available. Current plan includes hydration and salbutamol nebulizations. Levofloxacin will be started only if X-ray confirms pneumonia (sulfonamides contraindicated due to allergy). Reevaluation planned in 4-6 hours with results.
